youtube_items.title: [YT] SDG Report 2025 Overview - YouTube
youtube_items.summary: As we mark a decade since the  adoption of the 2030 agenda,   we stand at a turning point in human history. With just 5 years remaining, we've made  remarkable progress. But it's not enough.   The 2025 SDG Report reveals that only 35% of  SDG targets are on track or making moderate   progress with nearly half moving too slowly and  18% are regressing below 2015 baseline levels.   The last decade proves that transformation  is possible. Millions have gained access   to essential services. For the first time,  more than half of the world's population is   now covered by at least one social protection  benefit. Maternal and child mortality rates   have fallen. More than 100 million childre

youtube_items.summary: [DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] Secondly, they integrate all dimensions of sustainability: economic development, social progress and environmental protection. Thirdly, the SDGs tell us we should leave no one behind.

key_events: ‘Real impact on the ground’ needed as Indonesia backs UN reform
Iran crisis shows fossil fuels-based energy system ‘is inherently volatile and unstable’
AI in advertising risks fuelling information crisis, UN warns

raw_items.title: ‘Real impact on the ground’ needed as Indonesia backs UN reform
raw_items.summary:
Indonesia is expecting a “strengthened multilateral system that delivers real impact on the ground,” as one of the key outcomes of the ongoing reform of the United Nations, that’s according to the country’s outgoing Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs for Multilateral Cooperation, Tri Tharyat.

The post ‘Real impact on the ground’ needed as Indonesia backs UN reform appeared first on

raw_items.title: Iran crisis shows fossil fuels-based energy system ‘is inherently volatile and unstable’
raw_items.summary:
Senior UN climate leaders are getting behind countries mobilising this week for a transition away from fossil fuels at a groundbreaking event in Colombia. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Iran crisis shows fossil fuels-based energy system ‘is inherently volatile and unstable’ appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

raw_items.title: AI in advertising risks fuelling information crisis, UN warns
raw_items.summary:
With spending on advertising topping $1 trillion a year worldwide, the United Nations on Wednesday highlighted the untapped power of major brands to shape the future of Artificial Intelligence, warning that a failure to act could deepen a global information integrity crisis.

The post AI in advertising risks fuelling information crisis, UN warns appeared first on Unit

raw_items.title: World News in Brief: Europe warming twice as fast, acute hunger in Lebanon, peacekeepers deployed in Congo
raw_items.summary:
New data shows record heatwaves from the Mediterranean to the Arctic last year, with glaciers shrinking and snow cover declining, according to the UN World Meteorological Organization (WMO).  Read Full Story on UN News
